input_messages.content: {"suggestions": ["Apply VLAN segmentation specifically on the interfaces connecting the compromised host (UbuntuComputer on cisco-switch-2 Gi0/1) and implement Layer 3 ACLs or firewall policies on routers to restrict inter-VLAN routing to sensitive network segments.", "Deploy host-based firewall or packet filtering rules on the UbuntuComputer to limit its ability to perform network scans and limit outbound scanning traffic to unauthorized IP ranges and ports.", "Enhance network-level anomaly detection by ensuring Snort IDS/IPS devices are deployed inline at key aggregation points including between cisco-switch-2 and cisco-router-R2 to detect and block reconnaissance attempts from the compromised host segment."], "chosen_mitigation": "Apply VLAN segmentation specifically on the interfaces connecting the compromised host (UbuntuComputer on cisco-switch-2 Gi0/1) and implement Layer 3 ACLs or firewall policies on routers to restrict inter-VLAN routing to sensitive network segments.", "no_mitigation_left": false, "current_mitigations_counter": 3, "reached_mitigation_limit": false}

response.content:
{
  "mitigation_currently_implemented": "Apply VLAN segmentation specifically on the interfaces connecting the compromised host (UbuntuComputer on cisco-switch-2 Gi0/1) and implement Layer 3 ACLs or firewall policies on routers to restrict inter-VLAN routing to sensitive network segments.",
  "comments": "I created VLAN 20 named 'Compromised_Host_Segregated' on cisco-switch-2 and assigned interface Gi0/1 (where the UbuntuComputer is connected) to this VLAN to segregate the compromised host traffic. Then on cisco-router-R2, I created an extended access control list named BLOCK_COMPROMISED to deny any IP traffic from the subnet 192.168.2.0/24 (compromised host network) to any destination, effectively blocking inter-VLAN routing or access to other sensitive network segments. This ACL was applied inbound on the e0 interface. This network segmentation and ACL restrict lateral movement and scanning capability of the compromised host, helping mitigate the ongoing OS scan attack while keeping the host in the network.",
  "device_identifier": "cisco-switch-2",
  "commands": [
    "conf t
vlan 20
name Compromised_Host_Segregated
exit
int Gi0/1
switchport access vlan 20
exit
end",
    "conf t
ip access-list extended BLOCK_COMPROMISED
deny ip 192.168.2.0 0.0.0.255 any
permit ip any any
exit
int e0
ip access-group BLOCK_COMPROMISED in
end"
  ]
}

additional_info.chosen_mitigation: Apply VLAN segmentation specifically on the interfaces connecting the compromised host (UbuntuComputer on cisco-switch-2 Gi0/1) and implement Layer 3 ACLs or firewall policies on routers to restrict inter-VLAN routing to sensitive network segments.
